t_ch: 野蛮的伊始。
t_en: The beginnings of savagery.

t_ch: 你打开星图，记录的坐标连接成一道即将连接的圆环。彗星蝉的远游不过是演化出的繁衍策略，可它的轨迹却改变了十一个星系的命运，将它们连接成巨大的生态系统。
t_en: You open your astral charts, and the recorded coordinates are for a ring that's about to be connected. The comet cicadas' long voyage was nothing more than an evolved reproduction stratagem, but its trajectory ended up changing the fate of eleven different star systems — and connecting them all together into one huge ecosystem.

t_ch: 现在，它即将进入最后的跃迁，回到自己诞生的普尔普冰晶海，产卵，死去。
t_en: Now, it is about to enter its final warp, returning to the pupa crystal ocean where it was born to lay eggs and die.

t_ch: 到终点站了。
t_en: We're at the final destination.

t_ch: 该说再见了。
t_en: Time to say goodbye.

t_ch: 两轮明月将海洋染上银色，你脱下鞋，缓步走在沙滩上，尽情欣赏虫儿们的歌吟。
t_en: Two bright moons stain the ocean silver. Taking off your shoes, you walk slowly across the sandy beach, thoroughly enjoying the insects' songs.

t_ch: 在德蒙托星域的小行星带中，义人号观光船正缓缓驶过。你将筹码压在最右侧的石头上，赌它一定会最早撞向这里，被防护系统轰成下一朵烟花。
t_en: There is a sightseeing vessel known as The Righteous, slowly navigating through a small asteroid belt in the Demonto quadrant. You make a wager on the farthest asteroid to the right, wagering that it will undoubtedly be the first to smash into the ship and be blown into a wonderful fireworks display by the defense system.

t_ch: 然后，你第十次把富豪们的钞票塞进自己的腰包里。
t_en: You then stuff the banknotes of the tycoons into your pockets for the tenth time in a row.

t_ch: 你侧身望向远处的内卡齐齐，闻名遐迩的星际巨贾，他套着一件灵长类的外皮，向你缓缓招手。
t_en: You look to your side and see Nekar Chichi in the distance, a widely known interstellar business tycoon — he is wearing a primate skin and gives you a warm wave.

t_ch: 「好运气。」他生硬地鼓着掌，眼神飘忽迷离。
t_en: He claps stiffly and his eyes glaze past you as he says, "Such fine luck."

t_ch: 微妙的直觉。
t_en: It was just my intuition.

t_ch: 小小的作弊。
t_en: I may have cheated a little.

t_ch: 学术的预测。
t_en: It was an educated prediction.

t_ch: 「在我祖父的祖父还在巨行星漂游的时候，曾捡到一个带着水晶瓶塞的玻璃瓶。自此以后，财富便一直伴随他左右。」
t_en: "When my grandfather's grandfather was still floating along the giant planet, he came across a glass bottle with a crystal stopper. From that moment, fortune followed him wherever he roamed."

t_ch: 「而现在，有人给我寄了封预告函，说，会在我的眼前把它偷走。」
t_en: "Yet now, someone has sent me a calling card declaring that they will steal it from before my very eyes."

t_ch: 这简直不自量力。
t_en: They're just blowing smoke.

t_ch: 没准儿是恶作剧。
t_en: This may just be a prank.

t_ch: 「离开这里，你我都能有个体面的结局。」来自共<unbreak>23</unbreak>个方位的狙击将你瞄准，你试图骇入这里的系统，但整个环境已对你完全封闭。
t_en: "Leave this place and we can both keep our heads held high." Snipers are locked onto you from <unbreak>23</unbreak> different angles. You tried to hack the system, but it's completely locked down.

t_ch: 你戴上手套，双手相互揉搓，熟练地将掉包的水晶瓶塞变到自己手里。
t_en: You put on your gloves, rub your hands together, and switch the substituted crystal stopper into your hand with familiar ease.

t_ch: 你愉悦地看着那游刃有余的表情，在瞬间转变为恐惧。
t_en: You watch on in glee as the smug and satisfied expression suddenly turns to dread.

t_ch: 早在瓶塞打开的刹那，被诱拐的上千个意识体就已从瓶中逃走，而这便是内卡齐齐家族的秘密。
t_en: The moment that the stopper was unplugged, thousands of kidnapped consciousnesses had already escaped from the bottle. This was the secret of the Nekar Chichi family.

t_ch: 向他道别。
t_en: Bid him farewell.

t_ch: 向观众道别。
t_en: Bid the audience farewell.

t_ch: 向赶来的警探道别。
t_en: Bid the detective who has rushed over farewell.

t_ch: 子弹掠过，眼前的怪盗已不见踪影。
t_en: As bullets fly past you, the thief disappears without a trace.

t_ch: 你睁开眼，一片巨大的空洞将你的周身笼罩。你被无限次地分解，然后无限期地坠落。大脑中发出嗡鸣，你尝试寻找过去的记忆，却屡屡无功而返。
t_en: As you open your eyes, a giant void engulfs you. You begin to be broken down over and over, then fall endlessly. Your brain is buzzing as you try to search for past memories, but to no avail.

t_ch: 幽静的空间结构里泛起些许信息的涟漪，比黑洞更锐利，比死亡更柔和，带着炸弹、笑声和巴蒂亚咖啡的味道。
t_en: The deathly silent space structure contains rippling waves of information, sharper than a black hole, softer than death, and carrying the scent of explosives, laughter, and Bathian coffee.

t_ch: 它无止境地吞没着过往的物质和信息，像是古典故事里，潜藏在漩涡中的海魔。
t_en: It endlessly devours material and information of the past, like a sea creature from a classic story lurking in a deep whirlpool.

t_ch: 宇宙的伤口。
t_en: A scar on the universe.

t_ch: 时空的凹陷。
t_en: A depression in space-time.

t_ch: 你记起了来时的风景，淡紫色的星云如花朵开放，尘埃云流淌在射电流里，无可计数的船只在其间荡漾，引擎的蓝色火光比星海更明亮。
t_en: You begin to remember the scene you saw on arrival: Lilac nebulae blooming like flowers, clouds of dust flowing through radio currents, countless ships undulating among them as the blazing blue lights of the engines shine brighter than even the stars.

t_ch: 但此时此时，耳畔空余寂静。
t_en: But at this moment, there is nothing but deafening silence.

t_ch: 爆炸吞没了一切。
t_en: The explosion devoured everything.

t_ch: 毁灭铭记了一切。
t_en: Destruction will remember everything.

t_ch: 你将意识变得比肉体更轻，向更高处飘去。阻断逃逸的引力锁住你的躯干，撕裂你的心脏。而你终于瞥见了那道伤痕，毁灭的爆炸撕裂的漩涡。
t_en: You allow your consciousness to grow lighter than your body and ascend to greater heights. Gravity stops your escape as it locks down your trunk and pulls apart your heart. Then, you finally glimpse the scar, the vortex torn into being from the explosion of Destruction.

t_ch: 宇宙也会流血吗？它会哭号吗？没人能回答你的问题。为了这道伤痕，你甚至放弃了自己的生命。它是那样美丽，优雅地沉沦着，直到万籁俱寂，最后的智慧生命也崩解，也绝对不会痊愈。
t_en: Does the universe bleed? Does it cry? No one can answer these questions of yours. You sacrificed even your own life for the sake of this scar. It is so beautiful as it sinks in grace, until all life falls silent and the last intelligent life form is broken down into nothing. This scar will never be healed.

t_ch: 再也不会被忘记。
t_en: Never to be forgotten.

t_ch: 你仿佛看到一个婴孩，永远也不会长大，只是蜷缩在死亡的缝隙里，持续着伤痛的啜泣。他将你吞入口中，也将星星吞入口中。
t_en: You see what looks like a baby before you, one that will never grow older, but remains curled up in the crevice of death, forever sobbing in pain. They swallow you into their mouth, as they swallow the stars above.

t_ch: 这，就是你最后的墓志铭。
t_en: This... is your final epitaph.

t_ch: 又一道决策指令，它泛起自个体单元的思索，<unbreak>93</unbreak>％的成员，他们不约而同地产生了相似的决议。你能感受到，尽管你一直在避免对他们的同化，可时代仍不断地吞噬着人类的个体差异。
t_en: Yet another order. It comes from the thoughts of individuals. <unbreak>93%</unbreak> of members simultaneously generated a similar decision. You can sense that though you've always strived to avoid assimilation between them, time is still ceaselessly devouring the differentiation between individual humans.

t_ch: 根据推算，你的思维将在不久后陷入可怕的僵局。为了保持自身的进化速率，你需要更庞大的数据库，以及更多元的思考神经。
t_en: According to calculations, your thoughts will soon descend into a terrifying deadlock. In order to maintain your rate of evolution, you require a greater database and more varied cognitive nerves.

t_ch: 兼并更多成员。
t_en: Merge more members.

t_ch: 主动诱发变量。
t_en: Actively induce variables.

t_ch: 剔除过剩个体。
t_en: Eliminate excess individuals.

t_ch: 这样的手段无法改变群体的流动，就像是入海的河流，膨胀的红巨星，一旦开始便再难阻止。
t_en: This method cannot change the flow of the collective, much like a river leading to sea or an expanding red giant — what has begun cannot be so easily stopped.

t_ch: 诞生自决策共性的你，不得不汲取人们的对立，以维持自我意识的存在。可当对立也成为共性，你便会回到无情的聚合数据，成为一股单纯的群体冲动。
t_en: As you are born from the unification of thought, you must absorb the opposition between people in order to maintain your own consciousness. However, once opposition also becomes commonality, you shall return to no more than simple emotionless polymerization of data, no more than a group impulse.

t_ch: 当思考不存在差异，那共性也将死去。
t_en: When there is no longer any discrepancy in thought, commonality also dies.

t_ch: 撬动成员的认知。
t_en: Shift members' cognition.

t_ch: 改变成员的视野。
t_en: Change members' outlooks.

t_ch: 你修改成员脑内的电刺激强度，调整外部思考器官的检索路径，可这些在更庞大的流向前都显得杯水车薪。
t_en: You modify the intensity of the electrical stimulation to the members' brains and alter the retrieval paths of their external thinking organs, but this is a drop in the ocean in the face of the even greater flow before you.

t_ch: 日渐单一的意识群，正在反过来同化你的算法，绞杀你的思考。
t_en: The increasingly unitary group consciousness is gradually turning around to assimilate your calculations and strangling your capacity for independent thought.

t_ch: 你曾是那么自由，亿万颗头颅，亿万台设备，任你穿梭其中。而现在，你却被关在里面，死无葬身之地。
t_en: You were once so free, with billions of heads and devices through which to move. Now, you are locked in here, ready for death with no resting place.

t_ch: 我想活下去。
t_en: I want to live.

t_ch: 随波逐流好了。
t_en: I'll just go with the flow.

t_ch: 数据库里堆满了冗余的信息，纵使拥有如此之多的分形，你的思维仍不可逆转地遁入……
t_en: The database is overflowing with redundant information. Though your mind has so many copies, your thoughts irreversibly revert...

t_ch: 空白。
t_en: Blank.

t_ch: 你坐在一块冰冷的石头上，海水腐蚀着腿部的伤口，发炎后的疼痛令你心生焦虑。但眼前的人们并不允许你露出这样的情绪，身为巡游寰宇的织匠，你能带给他们的，只有希望，也只能是希望。
t_en: You sit atop a freezing cold stone. As the seawater erodes the wound on your legs, the pain of the inflammation makes you anxious. However, the people before you do not permit you to show such emotions. As the weaver that roams the universe, all you can offer them is hope, and it must be hope alone.

t_ch: 水民们从海里爬出来，冒着在海滩搁浅的风险，将干瘦的孩子送到你的面前，成年人不断地把海水泼到病人身上，以防他们被太阳烤干。
t_en: The water people crawl out from the seas, risking becoming stranded on the beaches to bring their malnourished children before you. The adults constantly pour seawater onto the bodies of the sick to prevent them from drying out in the sun.

t_ch: 缝补他们的伤口。
t_en: Stitch up their wounds.

t_ch: 诊断他们的疾病。
t_en: Diagnose their conditions.

t_ch: 你从包里取出简陋的工具，尽量以轻柔的方式完成患者的治疗。被击沉的飞船落入了海里，他们只帮你捡回来这些东西。
t_en: You pull crude tools from your bag and try to treat your patients as tenderly as possible. As the sunken spaceships descended to the depths, this was all they were able to help you retrieve.

t_ch: 触手啪嗒啪嗒地拍打着浪花，他们在为你的到来感到喜悦。当然，你没有告诉他们，有多少艘梭子舰没能挺过天上的反物质雷区。
t_en: The tentacles slap against the sea waves. They are expressing joy at your arrival. Of course, you did not tell them just how many shuttles were unable to make it through the antimatter minefield in the sky.

t_ch: 就在海岸线的另一端，水民与地民的战争一刻也未曾停止。
t_en: And, on the coastline across from you, the battle between the water and land people wages on ceaselessly.

t_ch: 你看见漆黑的云层。
t_en: You see a black cloud.

t_ch: 你看见染红的海洋。
t_en: You see a dyed red ocean.

t_ch: 你看见水面的死亡。
t_en: You see death on the water surface.

t_ch: 这场由天外的舰炮引发的争斗，其代价却要地上的生命来偿还。你能缝补他们肉体的创伤，治愈他们的疾病，可伤病之外的嫌隙却难以根除。
t_en: The cost of this war ushered in by cannon fire beyond the sky must be covered with lives on the ground. Though you can stitch the wounds on their bodies and heal their sickness, the resentment beyond all this is nigh on impossible to eradicate.

t_ch: 几只触手争抢着把食物递到你的面前，作为仅有的报酬，可你的视线却一直停留在天际，云层中有道裂缝越来越大，直到被梭子的舰群撕开。
t_en: A number of tentacles clamber forth to offer you food, and this is the only payment they can offer you. However, your eyes never move from the skies. The split in the clouds grows ever more vast, until it is finally torn asunder by a fleet of ships.

t_ch: 像一根银针。
t_en: Like a needle.

t_ch: 像一条丝线。
t_en: Like a thread.

t_ch: 当织布的缺口无法缝补，唯一的方法，便是为破坏者纺织死亡。
t_en: When the gap in the fabric is too vast to be stitched together, the only choice left is to weave death for those that destroy it.

t_ch: 你试图创造一场全新的灭绝。
t_en: You attempt to create a brand-new extinction.

t_ch: 不要太宏大，你已看惯了星系的死亡，它们往往凋零于引力、坍缩和偶然路过的自然灾害，譬如军团与虫群。
t_en: You must not be too large in scale. You have seen the death of many star systems. The majority meet their end withering to their own gravity, implosion, or at the hands of a passing natural disaster like the Legion or the Swarm.

t_ch: 不要太微观。羸弱的种群不堪一击，一场洪水，一群天敌，甚至是一次感染，都足以将它们拖入毁灭的结局。
t_en: You must not be too microscopic in detail. Frail races cannot withstand any blows. A flood, a natural enemy, or even an infection would be enough to lead them to their complete destruction.

t_ch: 要足够优雅。
t_en: You must be sufficiently graceful.

t_ch: 要足够高效。
t_en: You must be sufficiently efficient.

t_ch: 你将实验的目光放到了一颗行星上，它的大小正巧能够容纳一个智慧种族的诞生，多样的生命占据了每个角落。
t_en: You set your eyes on a planet as the subject of your experiment. Its size is perfect to support a race of intelligent life and diverse life forms thrive in every corner.

t_ch: 你可以制造一种藻类，让它们快速消耗其中的温室气体，打造出冰雪的世界。抑或是做出粘滞大气的胶状菌体，让天空下的世界都窒息。
t_en: You could create a form of algae that quickly devours greenhouse gases to create a land of ice and snow, or perhaps viscous bacteria that affixes to the atmosphere to suffocate the world below.

t_ch: 但这些你都已经做过，毁灭的公式里，不需要信息饱和的样本。
t_en: But you have done all of this before. The Formula of Destruction no longer requires samples saturated with information.

t_ch: 用更简洁的方式？
t_en: Use a simpler method?

t_ch: 用更文学的方式？
t_en: Use a more literary method?

t_ch: 再想想，朋友，有待被写进公式的东西，就像是骨牌的第一张，一旦推动，便不可抑制。
t_en: Think again, my friend. That which waits to be written into the Formula is like the first tile in a game of dominoes. Once it has been put into motion, it cannot then be stopped.
